records.ety: [F. changer, fr. LL. cambiare, to exchange, barter, L. cambire. Cf. Cambial.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Changed (chānjd); p. pr. & vb. n. Changing.]

defs: To alter; to make different; to cause to pass from one state to another; as, to change the position, character, or appearance of a thing; to change the countenance.
quotes: Therefore will I change their glory into shame.

defs: To alter by substituting something else for, or by giving up for something else; as, to change the clothes; to change one's occupation; to change one's intention.
quotes: They that do change old love for new, Pray gods, they change for worse!

defs: To give and take reciprocally; to exchange; -- followed by with; as, to change place, or hats, or money, with another.
quotes: Look upon those thousands with whom thou wouldst not, for any interest, change thy fortune and condition.

defs: Specifically: To give, or receive, smaller denominations of money (technically called change) for; as, to change a gold coin or a bank bill.
quotes: He pulled out a thirty-pound note and bid me change it.

defs: To be altered; to undergo variation; as, men sometimes change for the better.
quotes: For I am Lord, I change not.

defs: To pass from one phase to another; as, the moon changes to-morrow night.

records.ety: [F. change, fr. changer. See Change. v. t.]

defs: Any variation or alteration; a passing from one state or form to another; as, a change of countenance; a change of habits or principles.
quotes: Apprehensions of a change of dynasty.
All the days of my appointed time will I wait, till my change come.

defs: A succession or substitution of one thing in the place of another; a difference; novelty; variety; as, a change of seasons.
quotes: Our fathers did for change to France repair.

defs: A passing from one phase to another; as, a change of the moon.
Alteration in the order of a series; permutation.
That which makes a variety, or may be substituted for another.

Small money; the money by means of which the larger coins and bank bills are made available in small dealings; hence, the balance returned when payment is tendered by a coin or note exceeding the sum due.
A place where merchants and others meet to transact business; a building appropriated for mercantile transactions.

quotes: They call an alehouse a change.

defs: Any order in which a number of bells are struck, other than that of the diatonic scale.
quotes: Four bells admit twenty-four changes in ringing.
